headline: Propane and Nostalgia: 'King of the Hill' Revival Blazes Back to Streaming
lede: Mike Judge's beloved animated comedy returns to Hulu this summer, promising fans a hilarious trip back to Arlen, Texas. The revival picks up right where fans left off, with Hank Hill and crew ready to tackle a new generation of suburban hijinks.
body:
## The Return of Suburban Satire

Hulu is set to resurrect the cult classic animated series *King of the Hill* with a fresh batch of 10 episodes dropping on July 20. The revival signals a triumphant comeback for one of television's most understated comedy gems, promising to blend nostalgic charm with contemporary cultural commentary.

### A Familiar Landscape, New Narratives

Series creator Mike Judge returns to the helm, bringing his signature dry wit and keen eye for middle-class American dynamics. The show's revival comes at a moment when audiences are craving intelligent, character-driven comedy that doesn't rely on cheap gags or overwrought pop culture references.

### What to Expect

While plot details remain tightly under wraps, early indicators suggest the new season will explore the evolving landscape of suburban Texas life. Fans can anticipate the same razor-sharp observations about family, work, and community that made the original series a critical darling.

The streaming strategy represents a calculated bet by Hulu to attract both longtime fans and a new generation of viewers curious about the show's legendary status. By releasing all episodes at once, the platform allows for binge-watching and sustained audience engagement.
key_facts: Original series cast expected to return
